lessons.location: Jiefang Park (解放公园), Jiang'an District
learningGoals: Identify and name 3 different wetland plants in English
Understand why some plants float while others sink
Observe and draw plant structures (leaves, stems, roots)
lessons.activitySummary: Students will go on a 'Plant Scavenger Hunt' to find specific leaves and flowers near the park ponds. They will then conduct a simple 'Float or Sink' experiment using natural materials found on the ground to understand plant buoyancy.
lessons.safetyNotes: Keep students at least 2 meters away from the water's edge; use long-handled nets for any plant collection; watch for slippery moss.

lessons.location: Sha Lake Park (沙湖公园), Wuchang District
learningGoals: Count birds and use numbers 1-20 in a real-world context
Identify bird body parts and basic movements (fly, swim, walk)
Understand the concept of a 'habitat' as a home for animals
lessons.activitySummary: Using child-friendly binoculars, students will participate in a 'Bird Census' to count how many white egrets and ducks they see. They will then use clay and twigs to build a model nest based on their observations of the environment.
lessons.safetyNotes: Ensure students do not feed the birds; stay on the designated wooden boardwalks; use sun protection in open areas.

lessons.location: East Lake Wetland Park (东湖湿地公园 - 落雁景区), Hongshan District
learningGoals: Use magnifying glasses to observe small insects and fish
Use adjectives (big, small, fast, slow) to describe aquatic life
Learn the life cycle of a frog or dragonfly through observation
lessons.activitySummary: Students will use magnifying jars to safely observe water samples and identify 'mini-beasts' like water striders or small fry. They will create a 'Water Life Map' by drawing the creatures they find and labeling their speed and size.
lessons.safetyNotes: Strict supervision near the lake shore; ensure students wash hands after touching pond water; watch for stinging insects.

lessons.location: Moon Lake Park (月湖公园), Hanyang District
learningGoals: Understand that wetlands help clean our city's water
Follow a 3-step English instruction to build a simple filter
Express simple ways to 'help' the earth (e.g., 'Do not litter')
lessons.activitySummary: Students will act as 'Engineers' to build a natural water filter using sand, pebbles, and charcoal in a plastic bottle. They will test how the wetland-inspired layers can turn 'cloudy' water into 'clear' water and discuss how to protect the lake.
lessons.safetyNotes: Remind students that the filtered water is for observation only and NOT for drinking; handle rocks and sand carefully to avoid eye contact.
